name: Clay County State Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Clay County, Illinois for over 100 years with personal service, multiple loan types, and core banking products.
description_long:
Clay County State Bank is a locally-owned community bank headquartered in Louisville, Illinois, that has served residents of Clay County and surrounding areas for over a century. The bank emphasizes personal, relationship-based banking with the tagline "Small enough to know you, Large enough to serve you." Their physical location is on the town square in Louisville, creating a traditional community banking presence.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of banking services including checking and savings accounts, CDs and IRAs, bill pay, mobile and text banking, and debit cards. Their lending portfolio includes farm loans, consumer loans, business loans, and residential real estate mortgages. As a locally-owned institution, they highlight quick loan approval processes due to local decision-making. Additional services include wire transfers, cashier's checks, money orders, automatic funds transfers, bank-by-mail, notary services, and Illinois license sticker sales.

What distinguishes Clay County State Bank is its emphasis on community relationships and local decision-making power for loan approvals. The bank maintains traditional banking hours and physical locations while also offering modern conveniences like online banking, mobile apps for both Android and iPhone, 24-hour ATM access, and outside night depository services. Their routing number is 081209985, and they provide financial calculators and current rate information on their website.

The bank serves a specific geographic market and operates as a traditional community institution rather than a digital-first or national lender. Customers should expect personalized service and the ability to work directly with local bank representatives, though the bank's service area appears primarily concentrated in Clay County, Illinois. This model works best for local residents seeking relationship-based banking rather than those seeking specialized services or national scale. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Over 100 years of established community banking history and local reputation
Quick loan approval process due to local, in-house decision-making rather than remote underwriting
Multiple loan types available including farm loans, consumer loans, business loans, and residential real estate
24-hour ATM and night depository for after-hours access to banking services
Modern digital banking options including online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and mobile/text banking
Additional convenience services like wire transfers, cashier's checks, notary services, and Illinois license sticker sales
Personal, relationship-based service with accessible local staff on the town square in Louisville
cons: Limited geographic service area concentrated in Clay County, Illinois, making it unsuitable for customers outside the region
No indication of competitive interest rates or rate comparison tools on website for deposit or loan products
Limited hours of operation (lobby hours end at 4 PM weekdays except Friday) may not accommodate all customer schedules
No mention of credit cards, investment services, wealth management, or business cash management beyond basic offerings
Smaller institution may have fewer branches and ATMs compared to regional or national banks
best_for: Clay County, Illinois residents seeking personalized, relationship-based community banking with local decision-makers
Farm owners and agricultural businesses needing specialized farm lending with quick local approval
Small business owners in the area wanting to work directly with local lenders rather than remote institutions
Customers prioritizing personal service and knowing their banker over digital-first or self-service banking models
diagnosis: Clay County State Bank is best for residents of Clay County, Illinois and surrounding areas seeking traditional community banking with personal relationships and local loan decision-making. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank is not accessible to customers outside its service area, and those seeking specialized services, investment products, or premium banking features should look elsewhere.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
